definition: a band of tissue in the body that contracts to produce movement
hook: There is a small creature living under your skin.
modernAnchor: You flex your bicep. You feel it move. You probably never asked what to call it.
journey: Roman anatomists watched bare arms tense and saw something familiar moving under the skin.
The bulge that rolled and twitched looked exactly like a small mouse darting beneath a sheet.
So they named it after the animal. Musculus. Little mouse. The name stuck for two thousand years.
Every time you flex your arm, you are watching a Latin rodent move.
